title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXI. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old woman toss'd up in a basket
            Nineteen times as high as the moon;
        Where she was going I couldn't but ask it,
            For in her hand she carried a broom.

        Old woman, old woman, old woman, quoth I,
            O whither, O whither, O whither, so high?
        To brush the cobwebs off the sky!
            Shall I go with thee? Aye, by and by.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCLXI. Gaffers And Gammers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
